Implemented: Render() now templates install.image from config via Go text/template ({{ .InstallImage }} = factory.talos.dev/metal-installer/<EffectiveSchematic>:<TalosVersion>). Templates updated, unit test covers cluster-default + per-node schematic. go build + go test ./... pass.

Implemented nostos upgrade: pure planner (ParseVersion/ComputePath adjacent-minor/OrderNodes worker-first) + injectable GitHub catalog + DetectVersion + health-gated execution. 33 tests pass. Verified end-to-end against live cluster: 'upgrade --to v1.13.3 --dry-run' auto-computed steps v1.11.6 -> v1.12.8 -> v1.13.3, controlplane last.

Implemented internal/upgrade/tui (Bubble Tea v2/Lipgloss v2) + shared upgrade.BuildPlan. TUI launches only on TTY; --dry-run/--yes/--output json paths preserved. 239 tests pass; verified live: default target=no-op, --to v1.13.3 computes v1.11.6->v1.12.8->v1.13.3.

config.yaml bumped to v1.13.3 + new schematics (amd64 8f04ea6b, arm64 6f9371bc) adding iscsi-tools+util-linux-tools. Verified render emits correct image per node. Templates already use {{ .InstallImage }} so no template edits needed.
Version-matched talosctl per hop implemented + parseClientVersion fixed for real --short output. Verified live: full rolling upgrade succeeded across all 3 nodes v1.10.3->v1.11.6->v1.12.8->v1.13.3.

Executed: nostos upgrade --to v1.13.3 --yes completed cleanly. Tool health-gated each node to v1.13.3 (worker-first, dell01 control plane last). Log: all 3 nodes '✓ healthy at v1.13.3' then '✓ cluster upgraded to v1.13.3'.

Longhorn VERIFIED WORKING after upgrade: iscsi-tools present on v1.13.3, longhorn-manager + instance-managers Running (no more iscsiadm crash), both tp1+tp4 Ready+Schedulable. Proven in production: Harbor (20Gi registry+DBs) and hermes have BOUND longhorn/longhorn-ha PVCs. Data-path optimization deferred to follow-up (live volumes present).

Talos v1.10.3->v1.13.3 upgrade complete on all 3 nodes (verified via kubectl OS-IMAGE v1.13.3, kernel 6.18.33). iscsi-tools+util-linux-tools schematics in place. nostos made UX-first: render-from-config (qfn.1), smart upgrade cmd (qfn.2), TUI (qfn.3), config bump (qfn.4), version-matched client (qfn.7), rolling upgrade executed (qfn.5). Longhorn functional + serving production PVCs (qfn.6).

Root cause: bootstrap_test.go set XDG_DATA_HOME but TalosDir()=~/.talos uses UserHomeDir (ignores XDG), so it wrote a stub to the real ~/.talos/config+kubeconfig each 'go test ./...'. Fix: t.Setenv(HOME, tmp). Verified full suite leaves real config md5 unchanged.

DONE. Capacity fixed across the cluster. tp1: migrated to 256GB NVMe (/var/mnt/storage) via live disk eviction, zero data loss. dell01: wiped its 256GB SATA (old Windows install), mounted at /var/mnt/storage via Talos disks config (reboot applied), added Longhorn control-plane tolerations -> joined with 255GB. tp4 stays 29GB (no extra disk - hw limit). Total ~539GB, two 255GB nodes enable real longhorn-ha replication. Fixed GitOps bug: duplicate defaultSettings block dropped defaultDataPath (was silently using /var/lib/longhorn). All 6 volumes healthy, app Healthy. Commits pushed.

All local-path storage migrated to Longhorn. home-assistant + zigbee2mqtt: data rsynced to Longhorn PVCs (zero loss; zigbee crashloop is pre-existing coordinator issue). bind9: recreated fresh (data thrown away per user; external-dns repopulates). foundry: recreated fresh on longhorn (was defunct/Pending, GPU node gone). echotube: PVC removed, uses emptyDir. Static local PVs (volumes-tp1.yaml) deleted. Verified: 0 local-path PVCs/PVs cluster-wide, all 10 PVs on longhorn/longhorn-ha, local-path-provisioner gone, no local-path StorageClass.

Core ArgoCD components running on tp1/tp4. Service networking on dell01 fixed - Tailscale routes updated. Dex-server, repo-server, notifications all healthy.

Dell01 service networking FIXED. Hermes Running on dell01. Home-assistant starting (image pulling). Foundry remains pending due to missing GPU node - that's a separate infrastructure issue, not a storage/CSI issue.
rpi01 removed from cluster. Node was drained (pods evicted) and deleted from Kubernetes. Cluster now shows 3 nodes: dell01, tp1, tp4.

Added 'AI-agent provisioning guardrails' section to .submodules/nostos/AGENTS.md (5 rules + rationale + observable-path pointer to pxe status/doctor/--log-json) and a terse HARD-RULES pointer subsection + Lesson learned (2026-06-06) to home-systems/AGENTS.md.

Decision recorded in notes: skip Linux CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE rootless path; standardize on sudo/sudoers + structured sudo_required error everywhere. Revisit only if nostos gains a Linux daemon mode (then systemd AmbientCapabilities, not binary setcap).

serve.go: detectNetworks() finds all up/non-virtual/RFC1918 interfaces; filterGatewayCollisions() drops any NIC whose IP equals its .1 gateway (the postmortem bug). buildDnsmasqArgs() (pure, unit-tested) emits one --interface + one proxy --dhcp-range per NIC, stage-1 --dhcp-boot=tag:!ipxe,ipxe.efi with NO server addr (so dnsmasq uses the arrival-interface IP as next-server), and per-interface stage-2 HTTP boot URLs keyed on dnsmasq's auto-set interface-name tag. --bind-interfaces restricts reply sockets. Server.Interface override honored (warns on gateway collision). 24 tests pass; full suite 222 pass.

serve.go: httpRequests now chan HTTPRequest{Path,SourceIP}; loggingMiddleware stamps source IP from RemoteAddr; added Server.LocalIPs() (loopback + bound interface IPs). provisioner/pxe Boot tap snapshots LocalIPs and tracks boot-chain source IPs; config-fetched is gated by pure helper shouldEmitConfigFetched(req, localIPs, bootChainIPs) = not-empty AND not-local/loopback AND previously fetched boot chain. An operator-host curl no longer trips it. Tests: TestLocalIPs*, TestLoggingMiddlewareStampsSourceIP*, TestShouldEmitConfigFetched (operator/node/unknown cases). Full suite 232 pass.

Added 'nostos pxe setup' (subcommand of pxe). New internal/pxe/setup.go: SudoersDropInContent (pure) emits scoped NOPASSWD drop-in for the dnsmasq binary (any args) + pkill -f dnsmasq; SudoersDropInPath=/etc/sudoers.d/nostos-pxe; SudoersInstalled()/sudoersInstalledAt(path,marker) (injectable path for tests); InstallSudoers orchestrates tmpfile 0600 -> visudo -cf -> sudo install -m0440 -o root -g wheel -> reverify, with typed errs.Validation/Auth/Internal+hints. Refactored serve.go startDnsmasq to share dnsmasqBinary(). schema Registry 'pxe.setup' (idempotent). dry-run emits canonical preview envelope (verified). setup_test.go is root-free. Full suite 238 pass.

Added 'nostos pxe doctor' (subcommand of pxe). New internal/pxe/doctor.go: RunDoctor(p,httpPort) DoctorReport{ok,interfaces,checks[]} with 7 checks: assets present, dnsmasq present, viable interfaces, gateway-collision (lists any .1-router-IP NIC via new candidateNetworks() pre-filter helper), HTTP port bindable, HTTP self-test fetch of /boot.ipxe over ephemeral port (200+non-empty), privileged-ports via SudoersInstalled(). Pass: emitObjectOutput single object, exit 0. Fail: typed errs.Validation with full report under details.report, single stdout object in json / human table in text, exit 10. Verified live: correctly flags missing sudoers. schema 'pxe.doctor'. doctor_test.go hermetic/root-free. Full suite 250 pass.

Added per-MAC PXE event observability. New internal/pxe/events.go: Phase constants (discover..ready) with ordinal ordering; Event{ts,mac,ip,phase,message}; EventStore appends NDJSON to <State>/pxe/events.ndjson (mutex, dir-create, missing-file tolerant, nil-safe); pure ClassifyHTTPPath (boot.ipxe->tftp, vmlinuz->kernel, initramfs->initramfs, /configs/<mac>.yaml->config+MAC); pure ParseDnsmasqLine (DISCOVER/REQUEST->discover, DHCPACK->tftp w/ IP+MAC binding, sent ipxe.efi->tftp); pure FoldState builds IP->MAC map from DHCPACK then relabels IP-only HTTP events onto the MAC, folding to NodeState at furthest phase. serve.go records at source in loggingMiddleware (NO two-consumer channel bug) + tees dnsmasq stdout/stderr via io.MultiWriter+line recorder; --log-json mirrors events to a file. 'nostos pxe status [NODE]' joins config by MACHyphen, list=NDJSON+table, single=object, --fields; unknown MACs surface known:no (seeds .8). schema pxe.status. events_test.go hermetic. Full suite 262 pass.

Fail-fast sudo + no-timeout daemon. serve.go: ErrSudoRequired sentinel; sudoOK(installed,nonInteractive) pure decision; CheckSudo()=sudoOK(SudoersInstalled(), sudo -n true ok); called in Server.Preflight() BEFORE the destructive wipe so missing sudoers aborts cleanly instead of wiping-then-hanging. CLI exit-12: newPxeCmd + up + node install map errors.Is(ErrSudoRequired)->errs.Auth.WithHint('run: nostos pxe setup'); errs.FromGo adds 'sudo required'->Auth. No-timeout: provisioner serveTO default 0 (WaitMaintenance skips kill timer, selects ctx/tap only), MaxWaitMaintenance()=0; orchestrate withDefaults no longer forces 10m/20m, chooseWaitTimeout returns 0=unbounded (BootTimeout no longer a fallback), waitCtx uses WithCancel when <=0; --serve-timeout default 0 (0=indefinite). Node can be powered on whenever; Ctrl+C still cancels. Tests: TestSudoOK, TestWaitMaintenanceUnbounded*, TestMaxWaitMaintenanceUnbounded, chooseWaitTimeout all-zero. gofmt-cleaned our files. Full suite 271 pass.

Unknown-MAC enrollment surfacing. events.go: Event/NodeState gain Interface; ParseDnsmasqLine extracts iface from DHCP(...) log lines; FoldState carries most-recent iface so unknown MACs show where they appeared. pxe status: enrollable = observed-via-events AND NOT known (never-observed config nodes stay known:yes/enrollable:false; only event-stream MACs absent from config get enrollable:true). JSON rows add interface+enrollable; text adds IFACE column + ENROLL marker; stderr-only actionable hint (text mode, >=1 enrollable) pointing to nostos node add -> node install; cobra Long documents the no-auto-provision enroll path. --fields extended (pxeStatusFields); schema pxe.status stdout includes interface+enrollable. events_test extended. gofmt-clean, full suite 271 pass.

Per-MAC installed->boot-from-disk. New internal/pxe/installed.go (MarkInstalled/ClearInstalled/IsInstalled, JSON at state/installed-macs.json, lowercased, missing-file tolerant; no cluster import). paths.InstalledMACs() added. serve.go startHTTP now uses ServeMux: dynamic /boot.ipxe handler (reads mac query; IsInstalled -> boot-from-disk iPXE 'exit' script that hands back to UEFI to boot local disk; else returns rendered install-chain bytes, 404 if missing) + static FileServer at /, whole mux wrapped in loggingMiddleware so events still recorded. buildDnsmasqArgs stage-2 URL now /boot.ipxe?mac=${mac:hexhyp} per interface. provisioner: Prepare ClearInstalled (every (re)install serves chain); Boot tap MarkInstalled at config-fetched (post-install reboot settles). No BIOS boot-order change, no never-settling loop. installed_test + serve handler tests + TestInstalledStateWiring. Full suite 279 pass, gofmt-clean.

All 10 child issues complete. nostos PXE is now reliable (multi-interface + gateway guard), sudo-less (pxe setup + fail-fast sudo_required), a no-timeout daemon, observable (NDJSON per-MAC events + pxe status + pxe doctor + --log-json), self-diagnosing, free of the false-config-fetch signal, settles installed nodes (boot-from-disk), surfaces unknown MACs for enrollment, and carries AI-workflow guardrail docs. Full suite 279 pass; build+vet clean.

Implemented as the flash provisioner module (internal/provisioner/flash) rather than a post-flash script. nostos node install rpi01 now dispatches to it: waits for maintenance, applies insecurely, bootstrap is idempotent for the joining CP.
etcd join complete: rpi01 promoted to voter, both members healthy. Cilium NotReady tracked separately.

Resolved by the interface fix. rpi01 NotReady was caused by no default route (template targeted eth0, but Pi4 NIC is end0), so the cilium agent had no internet to reach operator/apiserver. Fixed interface via MAC deviceSelector (hardwareAddr e4:5f:01:3c:68:fa); rpi01 now Ready, both etcd members healthy, quorum restored.
